wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the rodion.pro project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a personal website with soft cyberpunk aesthetics, covering its key features including multilingual support (Russian/English), five color themes, blog with MDX content, project showcase, auto-generated changelog, community features with Google OAuth, command palette navigation, and admin moderation tools. Document the overall architecture combining Astro 5.0 SSR with React islands, PostgreSQL database, and modern web technologies. Include the target audience, use cases, and positioning as a professional portfolio and blog platform. Provide high-level technology stack overview and explain how the different components work together to create a cohesive user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Comment System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the comment system component of rodion.pro. Document the hierarchical comment architecture with threading capabilities, including comment creation, deletion, and moderation workflows. Explain the flagging mechanism for user-reported content, hiding/unhiding functionality for moderation, and real-time comment updates. Detail the API endpoints for comment CRUD operations, including request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and validation rules. Cover the CommentsThread component implementation, including prop interfaces, rendering logic, and user interaction patterns. Document the comment moderation workflow for administrators, including content review processes and user management. Include security considerations such as input sanitization, rate limiting, and spam prevention measures. Provide practical examples of comment integration, styling customization, and extending comment functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the authentication system endpoints. Document the Google OAuth flow including the authorization start endpoint (/api/auth/google/start) and callback endpoint (/api/auth/google/callback). Detail the user context endpoint (/api/auth/me) for retrieving authenticated user information and the logout endpoint (/api/auth/logout) for session termination. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request parameters, response schemas, and authentication requirements. Include the OAuth flow sequence, state management, session handling, and security considerations. Provide practical examples of the complete authentication workflow, error handling scenarios, and integration guidelines for client applications. Address CSRF protection, token validation, and session management best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multilingual Content Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the multilingual content management system. Explain the internationalization architecture with Russian and English language support, URL routing strategy with language prefixes (/ru, /en), and content organization patterns. Document the translation key system, locale detection mechanisms, and alternate language linking. Cover the content structure for MDX blog posts in multiple languages, configuration options for adding new languages, and best practices for maintaining consistency across translations. Include examples of implementing new translated content, handling missing translations, and optimizing for SEO with hreflang attributes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interactive Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for interactive UI components in rodion.pro. Document the CommandPalette component including keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+K/Ctrl+K), search functionality, navigation commands, and theme switching capabilities. Cover the ThemeSwitch component with its five color themes (Soft Neon Teal, Violet Rain, Amber Terminal, Ice Cyan, Mono Green) and local storage persistence. Detail the LanguageSwitch component for multilingual navigation support. Include component props, event handling, accessibility features, keyboard navigation patterns, and integration with the overall application state. Provide usage examples showing initialization, customization options, and best practices for each interactive component.

wiki_catalogs.name: Comment System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the comment system including hierarchical threading architecture, comment CRUD operations, and moderation capabilities. Document the CommentItem component with recursive rendering, reply functionality, and user interaction patterns. Explain the comment data model with nested replies, visibility states, and reaction tracking. Cover the API endpoints for creating, updating, deleting, flagging, hiding, and unhiding comments. Include the frontend integration with local storage for draft persistence, real-time comment loading, and error handling strategies. Provide examples of implementing new comment features, handling deep nesting scenarios, and optimizing performance for large comment trees.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for rodion.pro development setup. Cover prerequisites including Node.js 20+, PostgreSQL 15+, and Google OAuth credentials. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including repository cloning, dependency installation with npm install, environment configuration by copying .env.example to .env, database setup with createdb and migration execution, and starting the development server. Include detailed environment variable configuration with SITE_URL, DATABASE_URL, GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET, DEPLOY_TOKEN, GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, ADMIN_EMAILS, and optional TURNSTILE variables. Document local development workflow, port configuration (localhost:4321), and basic troubleshooting for common setup issues. Provide clear examples and screenshots where helpful.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reaction System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the reaction system component of rodion.pro. Document the emoji-based reaction architecture, including reaction types, user interaction patterns, and real-time updates. Explain the reaction API endpoints for listing available reactions and toggling user reactions, including request/response schemas and authentication requirements. Detail the ReactionsBar component implementation, covering prop interfaces, rendering logic, and user interface patterns. Document reaction tracking mechanisms, including uniqueness constraints, user state management, and real-time count updates. Cover the reaction moderation workflow for administrators, including reaction filtering and user oversight. Include performance considerations such as efficient database queries, caching strategies, and real-time update mechanisms. Provide implementation examples for customizing reaction emojis, adding new reaction types, and integrating reactions with other community features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Comment API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the comment system endpoints. Document the comment creation endpoint (/api/comments) for submitting new comments, the comment flagging endpoint (/api/comments/[id]/flag) for user reporting, and the moderation endpoints (/api/comments/[id]/hide and /api/comments/[id]/unhide) for administrative content control. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and validation rules. Include comment threading structure, hierarchical reply management, and real-time update mechanisms. Provide examples of comment submission, moderation workflows, and error handling scenarios. Address rate limiting, spam prevention, and content filtering strategies. Document the relationship between frontend components and backend endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the user authentication system using Google OAuth 2.0. Explain the complete authentication flow from OAuth initiation to session management, including state handling, PKCE implementation, and security considerations. Document the session management architecture, user context resolution, and protected route handling. Cover the API endpoints for authentication, user profile retrieval, and logout functionality. Include configuration requirements for Google OAuth credentials, environment variables setup, and error handling strategies. Provide examples of integrating authentication into frontend components and handling authentication state in React applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for layout components that form the structural foundation of rodion.pro pages. Document the main Layout.astro component including page structure, content wrapping, and global styling integration. Detail the Header.astro component covering navigation elements, logo placement, responsive behavior, and integration with interactive components like CommandPalette and ThemeSwitch. Cover the Footer.astro component including copyright information, social links, and layout considerations. Document component composition patterns, prop interfaces, responsive design breakpoints, and styling approaches using Tailwind CSS classes. Include examples of layout customization, component nesting patterns, and integration with Astro's page routing system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reaction System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the emoji-based reaction system including reaction tracking, user-specific reaction management, and real-time updates. Document the ReactionsBar component with emoji selection, reaction counting, and user state management. Explain the reaction data model with target types and keys, unique user constraints, and reaction distribution tracking. Cover the API endpoints for listing available reactions and toggling user reactions. Include the frontend integration with optimistic updates, real-time synchronization, and user authentication requirements. Provide examples of extending the reaction system with new emoji types, implementing custom reaction behaviors, and handling concurrent reaction updates efficiently.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the rodion.pro system architecture. Describe the hybrid SSR/SSG approach using Astro 5.0 with Node adapter, explaining how static site generation works alongside server-side rendering for dynamic features. Document the technology stack including React 19 islands, TypeScript for type safety, Tailwind CSS for styling, PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM for data persistence, and Google OAuth 2.0 for authentication. Explain the file-based routing system, component architecture with Astro components and React islands, and the separation of concerns between static content (MDX), dynamic API endpoints, and database operations. Include system boundaries, data flow patterns, and integration points. Address the internationalization architecture with URL prefixes and content organization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Moderation Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the moderation tools of rodion.pro. Document the administrative moderation interface, including content review workflows, user management capabilities, and community governance features. Explain the moderation API endpoints for comment moderation actions (hide/unhide), user oversight tools, and content filtering mechanisms. Detail the moderation dashboard implementation, including user interface patterns, filtering options, and bulk moderation capabilities. Document moderation workflows for handling reported content, managing user accounts, and maintaining community standards. Cover security considerations for administrative access, audit logging, and moderation action tracking. Include best practices for community moderation, including content policy enforcement, user education, and escalation procedures. Provide implementation examples for extending moderation capabilities, adding new moderation tools, and integrating with external moderation services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reaction API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the reaction system endpoints. Document the reaction listing endpoint (/api/reactions) for retrieving emoji-based reactions and the reaction toggle endpoint (/api/reactions/toggle) for adding/removing user reactions. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and validation rules. Include the emoji selection system, reaction counting mechanisms, and real-time update patterns. Provide examples of reaction submission, removal workflows, and bulk reaction retrieval. Address reaction uniqueness constraints, rate limiting per user, and performance optimization for high-volume reactions. Document the integration with the frontend reactions bar component and real-time UI updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Portfolio Project Showcase
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the portfolio project showcase system. Explain the project data structure with fields for title, description, technologies, status (active/paused/archived), and links to website/GitHub/demo. Document the project display components, featured project selection logic, and filtering/sorting capabilities. Cover the integration between static project data and Astro pages for multilingual project listings. Include guidelines for adding new projects, managing project status changes, and customizing project cards. Provide examples of project data formatting, component usage patterns, and responsive design considerations for different screen sizes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Display Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for specialized display components used throughout rodion.pro. Document the ProjectCard.astro component including project data binding, image handling, status indicators, and link management. Cover the EventCard.astro component for displaying activity feeds, event types, timestamps, and interactive elements. Detail the TerminalHero.astro component showcasing terminal-style presentation, animated text effects, and responsive typography. Document the CommentsThread.tsx component including hierarchical comment rendering, user interactions, moderation controls, and real-time updates. Cover the ReactionsBar.tsx component for emoji-based reactions, user state management, and interactive feedback. Include component props, data structures, styling approaches, responsive behavior, and integration patterns with backend APIs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core features of rodion.pro platform. Cover the five key feature areas: multilingual content management with Russian/English support and URL routing, user authentication system using Google OAuth 2.0, portfolio project showcase with stack and status information, blog content management with MDX authoring and tagging, and community features including comment system with hierarchical threading and reaction system with emoji-based interactions. Explain how each feature contributes to the overall user experience and platform functionality. Document the implementation patterns, data models, and integration points between features. Include configuration options, customization possibilities, and usage examples for each core feature.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the webhook processing endpoints. Document the GitHub webhook endpoint (/api/webhooks/github) for processing GitHub events and the deploy event endpoint (/api/events/deploy) for manual deployment triggers. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request payload schemas, signature verification requirements, and response handling. Include supported GitHub event types, payload validation, security verification using HMAC signatures, and error handling for malformed requests. Provide examples of webhook configuration, event processing workflows, and integration with deployment pipelines. Address rate limiting, retry mechanisms, and webhook debugging techniques. Document the relationship between GitHub events and automated changelog generation.

Include guidelines for writing new blog posts, managing metadata, implementing tags/categories, and optimizing for SEO. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error handling. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Rodion.Pro - Personal Portfolio & Blog Platform

## Project Introduction

**Rodion.Pro** is a modern, full-stack personal portfolio and blog platform built with Astro 5.0, featuring internationalization, user authentication, interactive comments system, and administrative moderation tools. The project serves as both a professional portfolio showcasing projects and a technical blog with multilingual support.

### Purpose Statement
The platform provides a comprehensive digital presence for professionals, combining personal branding with technical content sharing while maintaining excellent performance and user experience across multiple languages.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Deliver a fast-loading, SEO-optimized portfolio website
- Support multilingual content (Russian and English)
- Implement robust user authentication and session management
- Provide interactive community features (comments, reactions)
- Enable efficient content moderation through admin interfaces
- Maintain clean, maintainable code architecture

### Target Audience
- Professional developers and tech enthusiasts
- Content creators seeking a modern blogging platform
- Individuals looking for a feature-rich portfolio solution
- Teams requiring a customizable content management system

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern static-first architecture with server-side rendering capabilities, utilizing Astro's hybrid approach for optimal performance and user experience.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Astro Server Runtime]
    B --> C[React Components]
    B --> D[Static Pages]
    B --> E[API Routes]
    
    E --> F[Database Layer]
    F --> G[(PostgreSQL)]
    
    H[Authentication] --> I[Session Management]
    I --> J[User Context]
    
    K[Internationalization] --> L[Multilingual Routing]
    L --> M[Content Localization]
    
    N[Build System] --> O[Server Adapter]
    O --> P[Node.js Runtime]
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of several key layers:

**Frontend Layer**: Astro-based static generation with React integration for interactive components
**API Layer**: Server-side routes handling authentication, comments, reactions, and moderation
**Data Layer**: PostgreSQL database with Drizzle ORM for type-safe database operations
**Authentication Layer**: OAuth 2.0 integration with Google and custom session management
**Content Layer**: MDX support for rich content authoring and internationalization

### Design Patterns

- **Repository Pattern**: Database operations abstracted through schema definitions
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic component creation for UI elements
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven moderation system
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable authentication providers
- **Template Method**: Consistent API route structure across endpoints

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The application uses Astro's conventional file structure with clear separation of concerns:

**Application Configuration**
- `astro.config.ts` - Central configuration for Astro, React integration, Tailwind CSS, and Sitemap generation
- `package.json` - Dependencies and build scripts including database migration commands

**Runtime Configuration**
- `src/env.d.ts` - TypeScript definitions for environment variables
- `.env.example` - Template for environment variable configuration

### Core Modules

**Database Layer** (`src/db/`)
- `index.ts` - Database connection management with connection pooling
- `schema/index.ts` - Complete database schema definition with relationships

**Authentication System** (`src/lib/auth.ts`)
- OAuth 2.0 implementation for Google Sign-In
- Session cookie management with security best practices
- Admin role validation system

**Session Management** (`src/lib/session.ts`)
- User context resolution from cookies
- Automatic session validation and expiration handling

### Configuration Approach

**Internationalization Setup**
- Dual language support (Russian/English) with automatic locale detection
- URL-based localization with configurable routing
- Content separation for different languages

**Build and Deployment**
- Server-side rendering with Node.js adapter
- Static site generation with dynamic API endpoints
- Environment-specific configuration management

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework Dependencies**
- `astro@^5.0.0` - Modern static site generator
- `@astrojs/react@^4.0.0` - React component integration
- `@astrojs/tailwind@^6.0.0` - Styling framework integration
- `@astrojs/mdx@^4.0.0` - Rich content authoring

**Database and ORM**
- `drizzle-orm@^0.38.0` - Type-safe database queries
- `postgres@^3.4.5` - PostgreSQL client with connection pooling

**UI and Interactivity**
- `react@^19.0.0` & `react-dom@^19.0.0` - Component framework
- `cmdk@^1.0.0` - Command palette functionality
- `nanoid@^5.0.0` - Secure random ID generation

### Integration Points

**External Services**
- Google OAuth 2.0 for authentication
- GitHub webhooks for deployment automation
- Cloudflare Turnstile for anti-bot protection

**Database Schema Relationships**

```mermaid
erDiagram
    USERS ||--o{ OAUTH_ACCOUNTS : "has"
    USERS ||--o{ SESSIONS : "creates"
    USERS ||--o{ COMMENTS : "writes"
    USERS ||--o{ REACTIONS : "gives"
    
    COMMENTS ||--o{ COMMENT_FLAGS : "flagged"
    COMMENTS ||--o{ COMMENTS : "replies"
    
    REACTIONS ||--|| USERS : "by"
    REACTIONS ||--|| COMMENTS : "on"
    
    EVENTS ||--o{ USERS : "created_by"
```

### Component Relationship Diagrams

**Authentication Flow**

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant AuthAPI
    participant Google
    participant DB
    
    Client->>+AuthAPI: GET /api/auth/google/start
    AuthAPI->>+Google: Redirect to OAuth
    Google-->>-AuthAPI: Authorization Code
    AuthAPI->>+Google: Exchange code for tokens
    Google-->>-AuthAPI: Access Token
    AuthAPI->>+DB: Create/Update user
    DB-->>-AuthAPI: User record
    AuthAPI->>+DB: Create session
    DB-->>-AuthAPI: Session ID
    AuthAPI-->>-Client: Redirect with cookie
```

**Comment System Architecture**

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Comment Form] --> B[API Route]
    B --> C[Database Validation]
    C --> D[Comments Table]
    D --> E[Reactions Lookup]
    E --> F[Response Tree]
    
    G[Moderation Panel] --> H[Hide/Unhide Actions]
    H --> D
    H --> I[Comment Flags]
    I --> D
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multilingual Content Management**
- Seamless switching between Russian and English interfaces
- URL-based localization with automatic language detection
- Separate content directories for each supported language

**Interactive Community Features**
- Hierarchical comment system with threading support
- Emoji-based reaction system (likes, excitement, etc.)
- Real-time moderation tools for administrators

**Professional Portfolio Showcase**
- Project showcase with detailed descriptions
- Resume/CV presentation with downloadable formats
- Technology stack visualization

### Implementation Highlights

**Advanced Authentication System** (`src/pages/api/auth/`)
- OAuth 2.0 integration with Google for seamless sign-in
- Secure session management with automatic expiration
- Admin-only access control for moderation features

**Dynamic Comment Engine** (`src/pages/api/comments/`)
- Real-time comment retrieval with nested threading
- Efficient database queries with reaction aggregation
- Comprehensive moderation workflow

**Intelligent Reaction System** (`src/pages/api/reactions/`)
- Emoji-based interaction with validation
- Automatic duplicate prevention
- Real-time reaction count updates

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

**Content Moderation Workflow**

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> CommentSubmitted
    CommentSubmitted --> UnderReview: Auto-moderation
    CommentSubmitted --> Flagged: User Report
    UnderReview --> Approved: No Issues
    UnderReview --> Hidden: Suspicious Content
    Flagged --> Hidden: Admin Decision
    Flagged --> Approved: Review Complete
    Hidden --> Approved: Content Updated
    Hidden --> Deleted: Severe Violation
    Approved --> [*]
    Deleted --> [*]
```

**User Interaction Flow**

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Access] --> B{Authenticated?}
    B -->|Yes| C[Full Features]
    B -->|No| D[Limited Features]
    
    C --> E[Write Comments]
    C --> F[Give Reactions]
    C --> G[Access Admin Panel]
    
    D --> H[Browse Content]
    D --> I[Read Only Mode]
    
    G --> J[Hide Comments]
    G --> K[Delete Content]
    G --> L[Manage Users]
```

**Performance Optimization Strategy**

The platform implements several optimization techniques:
- Server-side rendering with static generation for improved SEO
- Database connection pooling for efficient resource utilization
- Lazy loading for interactive components
- CDN-ready asset optimization
- Progressive enhancement for JavaScript-dependent features

**Security Measures**
- CSRF protection through state parameter validation
- Input sanitization and validation
- Role-based access control for administrative features
- Secure cookie configuration with HTTPS enforcement
- Rate limiting for API endpoints

This comprehensive architecture ensures scalability, maintainability, and an exceptional user experience while providing a solid foundation for future feature development.

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [astro.config.ts](astro.config.ts)
- [src/env.d.ts](src/env.d.ts)
- [.env.example](.env.example)
- [src/db/index.ts](src/db/index.ts)
- [src/db/schema/index.ts](src/db/schema/index.ts)
- [src/lib/auth.ts](src/lib/auth.ts)
- [src/lib/session.ts](src/lib/session.ts)
- [src/pages/api/auth/google/start.ts](src/pages/api/auth/google/start.ts)
- [src/pages/api/auth/google/callback.ts](src/pages/api/auth/google/callback.ts)
- [src/pages/api/comments/index.ts](src/pages/api/comments/index.ts)
- [src/pages/admin/moderation.astro](src/pages/admin/moderation.astro)
- [src/pages/api/comments/[id]/hide.ts](src/pages/api/comments/[id]/hide.ts)
- [src/pages/api/reactions/toggle.ts](src/pages/api/reactions/toggle.ts)
- [src/components/CommandPalette.tsx](src/components/CommandPalette.tsx)

</blog>
